text: hospital course: [patient_name] is a 85 y.o. female with past medical history of arthritis, hypertension, recurrent uti who was brought to the er for altered mental status. patient apparently was difficult to arouse today morning and was brought to the er. her temperatures were low and heating blanket was placed. patient's blood pressure was on the lower side and she received iv fluids. at time of my visit, patient was alert and oriented x 4. she reported dysuria and pain in hips. patient endorses pressure ulcers in the buttocks ulcers in lower extremities. patient apparently had a head injury about 10 days ago with a laceration to the face and had sutures placed. workup in er: uds positive for opiates; ua with leukocytes; chest x-ray with left basilar opacity -atelectasis versus pneumonia; ct head with no acute findings. hospitalist was called to admit patient. course: patient was admitted and started on iv antibiotics. wound care was consulted. blood and urine cultures were ordered and did not show any growth. since admission, patient has been doing well. she currently has no complaints. patient to be discharged today on oral antibiotics and will follow-up with pcp in clinic. she has been instructed to go to the er for any worsening symptoms.

text: hospital course: [patient_name] is a 85 y.o. female with past medical history of arthritis, hypertension, recurrent uti who was brought to the er for altered mental status. patient apparently was difficult to arouse today morning and was brought to the er. her temperatures were low and heating blanket was placed. patient's blood pressure was on the lower side and she received iv fluids. at time of my visit, patient was alert and oriented x 4. she reported dysuria and pain in hips. patient endorses pressure ulcers in the buttocks ulcers in lower extremities. patient apparently had a head injury about 10 days ago with a laceration to the face and had sutures placed. workup in er: uds positive for opiates; ua with leukocytes; chest x-ray with left basilar opacity -atelectasis versus pneumonia; ct head with no acute findings. hospitalist was called to admit patient. course: patient was admitted and started on iv antibiotics. wound care was consulted. blood and urine cultures were ordered and did not show any growth. since admission, patient has been doing well. she currently has no complaints. patient to be discharged today on oral antibiotics and will follow-up with pcp in clinic. she has been instructed to go to the er for any worsening symptoms.
